+---
+assignees:

+depends_on: []
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: dd80
+title: 'Commands: generalize paste to scope-chain-driven dispatch matrix (PasteEntityCmd)'
+---
+## What
+
+`PasteTaskCmd` in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/clipboard_commands.rs` has a small hard-coded dispatch: task-in-clipboard + column-in-scope → create task in column; tag-in-clipboard + task-in-scope → tag the task. Generalize to a full `(clipboard_type × scope_chain)` dispatch matrix. Paste's outcome is determined by *where* you paste, not just what's on the clipboard. Walking the scope chain gives the set of plausible destinations; `available()` and `execute()` pick the best one.
+
+### The dispatch principle
+
+Paste is target-driven but the target alone is insufficient. The scope chain provides the full context. `PasteEntityCmd::execute` walks the scope chain innermost-first and stops at the first moniker that produces a valid `(clipboard_type, target_type)` pair. `available()` returns true when at least one such pair exists.
+
+### Dispatch matrix (initial set)
+
+| Clipboard type | Target/scope moniker | Action |
+|----------------|----------------------|--------|
+| `task`         | `column:*` or `board:*` | Create a copy of the task in that column; for board, use the leftmost column. If cut, delete the source. |
+| `task`         | `project:*` | Create a copy of the task, set its `project` field to the target project. |
+| `tag`          | `task:*` | Add the tag to the task (tag-the-task, not create-a-copy). Ignore cut flag — tags aren't "moved" by paste. |
+| `tag`          | `tag:*` | Not applicable — return unavailable. |
+| `project`      | (any)       | Not applicable for v1 — return unavailable. Project copy semantics are unclear; defer. |
+| `column`       | (any)       | Not applicable for v1 — defer. |
+| `actor`        | (any)       | Not applicable for v1 — defer. |
+| `board`        | (any)       | Not applicable for v1 — defer. |
+
+Combinations not listed are `unavailable`. Start with the four supported combinations above; extend later as product decisions land.
+
+### Files to touch
+
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/clipboard_commands.rs` — rename `PasteTaskCmd` → `PasteEntityCmd`. Rewrite `available()` to consult the full scope chain + clipboard payload; rewrite `execute()` to dispatch on the matched pair.
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/mod.rs` — registration key stays `entity.paste`; update the struct name.
+- `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/entity.yaml` — `entity.paste` params change from `{name: column, from: scope_chain, entity_type: column}` to `{name: moniker, from: target}`. The Rust impl also reads the full scope chain for destination resolution — target is a hint, chain is the authority.
+
+### Subtasks
+
+- [ ] Define the dispatch matrix as a helper `resolve_paste_action(clipboard: &ClipboardPayload, scope_chain: &[String]) -> Option<PasteAction>`.
+- [ ] Rewrite `PasteEntityCmd::available` to call the helper and return `action.is_some()`.
+- [ ] Rewrite `PasteEntityCmd::execute` to dispatch on the matched action (create task in column, tag the task, create task with project field, etc.).
+- [ ] Update `entity.yaml` paste params to `from: target`.
+- [ ] Honor the `is_cut` flag — delete the source entity after a successful copy-on-paste.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] `PasteEntityCmd::available` returns true exactly when the dispatch matrix has an entry matching the clipboard + scope chain.
+- [ ] Paste a task onto a column → a new task exists in that column.
+- [ ] Paste a task onto a board (no column targeted) → a new task in the leftmost column.
+- [ ] Paste a task onto a project → a new task with `project: <id>` set.
+- [ ] Paste a tag onto a task → the task now has that tag.
+- [ ] Paste a cut task onto a column → new task exists; source task deleted.
+- [ ] Paste a tag onto a tag → unavailable; no-op; no error shown.
+- [ ] `entity.paste` in `entity.yaml` declares `from: target` with no scope pin.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] Add `paste_task_into_column_creates_copy`.
+- [ ] Add `paste_task_into_board_uses_leftmost_column`.
+- [ ] Add `paste_task_into_project_sets_project_field`.
+- [ ] Add `paste_tag_onto_task_adds_tag`.
+- [ ] Add `paste_cut_task_deletes_source`.
+- [ ] Add `paste_tag_onto_tag_is_unavailable` — assert `available: false`.
+- [ ] Add `paste_outcome_depends_on_scope_chain` — same clipboard payload, different scope chains produce different outcomes.
+- [ ] Run command: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban clipboard paste` — all green.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — write `paste_outcome_depends_on_scope_chain` first; it encodes the core principle and fails until the matrix dispatch lands.
+- Implement the helper `resolve_paste_action` table-driven so future matrix entries (e.g. paste-project-into-project) are additions, not refactors.
+
+#commands
+
+Depends on: 01KPERTKGC5KH0Z21SMKJ3Y4M5 (copy/cut generalization must land first so the clipboard payload carries structured data)
\ No newline at end of file

changes.value: Commands: generalize paste to scope-chain-driven dispatch matrix (PasteEntityCmd)

## What

`PasteTaskCmd` in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/clipboard_commands.rs` has a small hard-coded dispatch: task-in-clipboard + column-in-scope → create task in column; tag-in-clipboard + task-in-scope → tag the task. Generalize to a full `(clipboard_type × scope_chain)` dispatch matrix. Paste's outcome is determined by *where* you paste, not just what's on the clipboard. Walking the scope chain gives the set of plausible destinations; `available()` and `execute()` pick the best one.

### The dispatch principle

Paste is target-driven but the target alone is insufficient. The scope chain provides the full context. `PasteEntityCmd::execute` walks the scope chain innermost-first and stops at the first moniker that produces a valid `(clipboard_type, target_type)` pair. `available()` returns true when at least one such pair exists.

### Dispatch matrix (initial set)

| Clipboard type | Target/scope moniker | Action |
|----------------|----------------------|--------|
| `task`         | `column:*` or `board:*` | Create a copy of the task in that column; for board, use the leftmost column. If cut, delete the source. |
| `task`         | `project:*` | Create a copy of the task, set its `project` field to the target project. |
| `tag`          | `task:*` | Add the tag to the task (tag-the-task, not create-a-copy). Ignore cut flag — tags aren't "moved" by paste. |
| `tag`          | `tag:*` | Not applicable — return unavailable. |
| `project`      | (any)       | Not applicable for v1 — return unavailable. Project copy semantics are unclear; defer. |
| `column`       | (any)       | Not applicable for v1 — defer. |
| `actor`        | (any)       | Not applicable for v1 — defer. |
| `board`        | (any)       | Not applicable for v1 — defer. |

Combinations not listed are `unavailable`. Start with the four supported combinations above; extend later as product decisions land.

### Files to touch

- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/clipboard_commands.rs` — rename `PasteTaskCmd` → `PasteEntityCmd`. Rewrite `available()` to consult the full scope chain + clipboard payload; rewrite `execute()` to dispatch on the matched pair.
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/mod.rs` — registration key stays `entity.paste`; update the struct name.
- `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/entity.yaml` — `entity.paste` params change from `{name: column, from: scope_chain, entity_type: column}` to `{name: moniker, from: target}`. The Rust impl also reads the full scope chain for destination resolution — target is a hint, chain is the authority.

### Subtasks

- [ ] Define the dispatch matrix as a helper `resolve_paste_action(clipboard: &ClipboardPayload, scope_chain: &[String]) -> Option<PasteAction>`.
- [ ] Rewrite `PasteEntityCmd::available` to call the helper and return `action.is_some()`.
- [ ] Rewrite `PasteEntityCmd::execute` to dispatch on the matched action (create task in column, tag the task, create task with project field, etc.).
- [ ] Update `entity.yaml` paste params to `from: target`.
- [ ] Honor the `is_cut` flag — delete the source entity after a successful copy-on-paste.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `PasteEntityCmd::available` returns true exactly when the dispatch matrix has an entry matching the clipboard + scope chain.
- [ ] Paste a task onto a column → a new task exists in that column.
- [ ] Paste a task onto a board (no column targeted) → a new task in the leftmost column.
- [ ] Paste a task onto a project → a new task with `project: <id>` set.
- [ ] Paste a tag onto a task → the task now has that tag.
- [ ] Paste a cut task onto a column → new task exists; source task deleted.
- [ ] Paste a tag onto a tag → unavailable; no-op; no error shown.
- [ ] `entity.paste` in `entity.yaml` declares `from: target` with no scope pin.

## Tests

- [ ] Add `paste_task_into_column_creates_copy`.
- [ ] Add `paste_task_into_board_uses_leftmost_column`.
- [ ] Add `paste_task_into_project_sets_project_field`.
- [ ] Add `paste_tag_onto_task_adds_tag`.
- [ ] Add `paste_cut_task_deletes_source`.
- [ ] Add `paste_tag_onto_tag_is_unavailable` — assert `available: false`.
- [ ] Add `paste_outcome_depends_on_scope_chain` — same clipboard payload, different scope chains produce different outcomes.
- [ ] Run command: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban clipboard paste` — all green.

## Workflow

- Use `/tdd` — write `paste_outcome_depends_on_scope_chain` first; it encodes the core principle and fails until the matrix dispatch lands.
- Implement the helper `resolve_paste_action` table-driven so future matrix entries (e.g. paste-project-into-project) are additions, not refactors.

Walk the scope chain innermost-first; at each moniker, look up `(clipboard_type, target_type)` in the matrix; the first hit wins. Innermost moniker is most specific; if that pair isn't defined, unwind to the next level out.

+#[async_trait]
+pub trait PasteHandler: Send + Sync {
+    fn matches(&self) -> (&'static str, &'static str);
+    fn available(&self, clipboard: &ClipboardPayload, target: &str, ctx: &CommandContext) -> bool { true }
+    async fn execute(&self, clipboard: &ClipboardPayload, target: &str, ctx: &CommandContext) -> Result<Value>;
+}

+pub struct PasteMatrix {
